title: About
text:
Finance Grad 2026 | Commodity Analytics | Risk Management
Mathew is a finance graduate from the University of Alberta and aspiring energy trading analytics professional with experience developing quantitative supply-demand, uncertainty and scenario models, market dashboards, and actionable results for commodity market decision making. Proficient in R and Excel for building analytics tools and automating workflows. Strong interest in North American energy markets.
In his role at SGI, he automated over 50 annual hours spent on reporting data. Within 2 months of employment Mathew was tasked with leading and training his team of 9 colleagues through a major software transition.
In his first two weeks at CNRL, Mathew learned how to read engineering drawings from all disciplines and completed a LEM analysis of a project worth over $5 million. By the end of his work term, he was collaborating with engineers to sharpen estimates on projects worth over $50 million.
In his final two years of studies, Mathew has focused his learning on data science and risk management. He has earned an average GPA across 11 finance courses of 3.87/4.00 while working part-time as an insurance underwriting analyst, teaching assistant, and finance tutor.
Mathew is seeking opportunities to grow his skillset and work on impactful teams in the energy sector.

title: About
text:
Energy Finance Student | Commodity Analytics | Risk Management
Mathew is a fourth-year finance student at the University of Alberta and aspiring energy trading analytics professional with experience developing quantitative supply-demand, pricing, and scenario models for commodity market decision making. Proficient in R and Excel for building analytics tools and automating workflows. Strong interest in North American energy markets.
In his role at SGI, he automated over 50 annual hours spent on reporting data. Within 2 months of employment Mathew was tasked with leading and training his team of 9 colleagues through a major software transition.
In his first two weeks at CNRL, Mathew learned how to read engineering drawings from all disciplines and completed a LEM analysis of a project worth over $5 million. By the end of his work term, he was collaborating with engineers to sharpen estimates on projects worth over $50 million.
In his final two years of studies, Mathew has focused his learning on data science and risk management. He has earned an average GPA across 9 finance courses of 3.87/4.00 while working part-time as an insurance underwriting analyst, teaching assistant, and finance tutor.
Mathew is seeking opportunities to grow his skillset and work on impactful teams in the energy sector.

title: Projects
text:
A selection of data and analytics projects in energy finance and risk management.

Commodity Market Dynamics
An R Shiny dashboard for exploring commodity market structure across WTI crude, Brent crude, natural gas, RBOB gasoline, and heating oil. Features forward curve visualization in 2D, 3D, and yearly comparison views, implied convenience yield derived from the cost-of-carry model, realized volatility surfaces, STL seasonal decomposition, cross-commodity co-dynamics, minimum variance hedge ratio analysis, and EIA supply/demand balances. Packaged as a Docker image and deployed via Railway.
Finance 451: FinTech III at the University of Alberta.


Natural Gas Trading Simulation
A simulation-based trading strategy for Henry Hub natural gas futures driven by supply/demand fundamental analysis. Integrates EIA storage data, NOAA weather data (HDD/CDD), and seasonal patterns to construct and evaluate time spreads, location spreads, and inter-commodity spreads. Built in R with interactive Plotly visualizations.
Finance 440: Commodity Trading and Analytics at the University of Alberta.


WTI Options Portfolio: Full Greek P&L Attribution
A P&L attribution framework for a WTI crude oil options portfolio. Computes Delta, Gamma, Vega, Vanna, and Theta using central differences, evaluates delta-hedging strategies, and validates P&L attribution under simulated market moves.
Finance 452: Financial Trading and Analytics at the University of Alberta.


Crude Oil Trading Algorithm
An autonomous quantitative trading algorithm that generates buy/sell signals from 3-2-1 crack spread dynamics to speculate on WTI crude oil futures. Incorporates parameter optimization, position sizing, and commission/slippage modelling, with backtesting across volatile periods including COVID-19 and the Russia-Ukraine conflict. Live futures prices sourced via the Zema API.
Finance 440: Commodity Trading and Analytics at the University of Alberta.


Sector Rotation Trading Algorithm
A trading algorithm built in R, rotating across 11 SPDR sector ETFs using K-Means and hierarchical clustering to identify Cyclical and Defensive asset groupings. Features a Kalman Filter local-level model as an adaptive price baseline, with a 20-day SMA minus Kalman spread as a stress detection signal, monthly rebalancing, and equal-weight fallback during confirmed downtrends. Optimization via parallel grid search maximizing Omega Ratio subject to a Calmar floor, with COVID-period robustness testing.
Finance 452: Financial Trading and Analytics at the University of Alberta.


Algorithmic Eats
An R Shiny dashboard for macro-nutrient optimization and meal planning. Features a grocery optimizer using linear programming, a weight simulation tool, and a dynamic gym map powered by the Google Maps API. Packaged as a Docker image. Collaborative project.
Finance 451: FinTech III at the University of Alberta.


Fixed Income IR Management
An R Shiny dashboard for fixed income portfolio management. Displays historical US Treasury yield curves and forward rate curves sourced from FRED, a portfolio builder for bond positions, and Greek-based risk metrics. Implements bond pricing calculations in C++ via Rcpp. Collaborative project.
Finance 451: FinTech III at the University of Alberta.
